name: terminal
description: Execute commands. Supports building persistent environments (like keeping a real terminal tab open) or running background tasks.

timeout_sec.description: Execution timeout in seconds. 0 means no timeout. In background mode this caps the running process duration.

cwd.description: Optional path used as working directory. For PTY mode, this is relative to playground_root; otherwise it is resolved from the current working directory.

session_name.description: A 'name tag' for a persistent window (e.g. 'work'). When you use the same name, we reuse that specific window so it remembers your previous folder (cd) and variables (export) exactly.

background.description: Run in the background. Returns immediately and lets the task finish on its own. Use this for starting long-running processes. And it will notify you when done.

pty.description: Interactive mode. Creates a 'persistent window' and remembers your context. Use this if you want subsequent calls to continue where the last one left off.
